text:
Removed-behavior audit for PR #2866. The two headline concerns you flagged both clear: WalletConfig is NOT serde-derived (the kind:17375 content is a hand-built JSON [key,value] pair array; `relay` lives in event *tags*, not content), so `relays`→`legacy_relay_hint` is a pure Rust field rename with zero effect on serialized/persisted/wire shape — NIP-60 round-trip is unchanged. And the removed WalletBackend/PayResult/WalletError trait + re-exports have NO consumers anywhere in the checkout (only self-references and unrelated nmp-nwc `pay_invoice`), so nothing downstream breaks. Ownership.rs's 7 `exclusive:true` kind claims (17375/7375/7376/7374/10019/9321/38172) have no colliding claim in any other crate's ownership.rs, and the D26 protocol-crate test already listed nmp-nip60. Genuinely thin. Two low-confidence candidates below.

[
  {
    "file": "crates/nmp-nip60/src/nip60_wallet/nutzap_send.rs",
    "line": 84,
    "summary": "publish_nutzap_info still writes legacy_relay_hint into the kind:10019 relays field the diff's own docs declare non-authoritative",
    "failure_scenario": "Every doc comment touched by this PR (lib.rs, mint_announce.rs, wallet_event.rs, nip60_wallet.rs, and the legacy_relay_hint() accessor) newly and emphatically asserts the kind:17375 relay tags are legacy residue that 'must never' be used as relay-selection source of truth — the authoritative set is kind:10019/NIP-65. But publish_nutzap_info still populates the *published* kind:10019 NutZap-info `relays` straight from self.legacy_relay_hint. kind:10019 relays are the ONE place NIP-61 treats this data as authoritative: senders read the recipient's kind:10019 relays to know where to publish kind:9321 nutzaps. A wallet decoded via from_wallet_event from a kind:17375 that had no relay tags gets an empty legacy_relay_hint, so publish_nutzap_info emits a kind:10019 advertising an empty relay set, and inbound nutzaps become undeliverable — the user silently receives nothing. The behavior is unchanged from before the rename (pre-existing quirk, not a code regression), but the PR's new contract now directly contradicts this write path, so it's the highest-value thing to flag." [...]     "summary": "Rename is skin-deep on the WRITE path: freshly-built kind:17375 events still emit `relay` tags from `legacy_relay_hint`. The design doc's Relay Acquisition section sanctions only PARSING legacy tags ('parse it only as a non-authoritative compatibility hint'), i.e. read-compat for old wallets — not emitting new ones. `WalletConfig::generate(mints, legacy_relay_hint)` + `build_wallet_event` keep producing the exact residue activation was supposed to stop creating. Deeper fix: new kind:17375 should carry no relay tags (nmp-wallet owns relay acquisition); the constructor should not take a relay-hint param at all.", [...] The PR does what issue #2865 asked: returns `nmp-nip60` to workspace/CI membership (dropping the standalone `[workspace]` stub and inheriting workspace package fields), deletes the dead `WalletBackend` trait whose `pay_invoice` always failed, renames the kind:17375 relay data to `legacy_relay_hint` with non-authoritative framing throughout, and adds the ownership declaration and release classification the gates require. Eight finder angles and seven adversarial verifiers later: the mechanical work is solid — no serde/wire breakage (the 17375 content is hand-built JSON, tags carry the relays), zero consumers of the deleted trait, no ownership collisions, and every doctrine/manifest/file-size gate passes. Six findings survived verification.
